message:
feat(algorithms): add SimRank, Google matrix, second-order centrality, communicability/current-flow betweenness

Five new Rust algorithm implementations, all cross-validated against NX:

- simrank_similarity: iterative pairwise SimRank with convergence control,
  supports both undirected (neighbors) and directed (predecessors) graphs
- google_matrix: row-stochastic PageRank transition matrix with dangling
  node handling, for both undirected and directed graphs
- second_order_centrality: random-walk standard deviation of mean first
  passage times via power iteration + Gauss-Jordan matrix inversion
- communicability_betweenness_centrality: matrix exponential (Taylor series)
  based node removal impact on all-pairs communicability
- current_flow_betweenness_centrality: Laplacian pseudo-inverse based
  electrical current flow between all source-target pairs

Also fixes pre-existing clippy lints:
- QueueEntry/PartitionDict visibility narrowed to pub(crate)
- Removed needless borrows in iterator __next__ methods
- Suppressed unused variable warnings in node_link_graph signature

471 public Rust functions, 691 Rust tests, 37,259 lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
